productFP.statement: Secure emergency exit doors against unauthorized use while preserving unimpeded single-motion escape capability and reporting door status events to centralized monitoring — without mains power at the door
productFP.object: emergency exit doors against unauthorized use
productFP.context: in any building with mandated emergency exits, while simultaneously preserving unimpeded single-motion escape per EN 179 / EN 1125
differentiators: Mechanical alarm operates independently of electrical power (fail-safe under all fault conditions)
Single-motion escape compliant with both EN 179 (lever handle) and EN 1125 (panic bar) — dual-standard coverage
Wireless status reporting without mains wiring at the door point — retrofit-ready installation

commodityFP.statement: Prevent unauthorized use of emergency exit doors while maintaining unimpeded escape capability and alerting to door activation via audible alarm
commodityFP.reasoning: No 8-digit UNSPSC commodity precisely captures 'emergency exit door alarm guards with IoT monitoring.' Product sits at intersection of door hardware (46171500) and alarm/monitoring systems (46171600). Max confidence < 80% threshold. Custom product group selected using UNSPSC-style naming convention.
commodityFP.fpExtension: Monitor distributed safety-critical hardware wirelessly, detecting activation events and battery status without mains wiring at the device point, and aggregating status to a centralized building management system
fpExtensionDomains: Any fleet of distributed passive safety devices
